findingsNew.claim: Generated phase-parent spec scope text still emits private manifest vocabulary despite ADR-005/workflow-invariance cleanup requirements.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked the clean phase-parent manifest template and workflow-invariance scan scope; the template uses Sub-phase list, but create.sh substitutes child phase manifest into generated body text and the invariance test does not scaffold/scan that output.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The word manifest could be read generically as a phase listing, but this exact public wording class was called out for cleanup and the generated spec is AI-readable.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if maintainers explicitly allow child phase manifest in public scaffold output or if another generated-output gate already blocks this release.

spec_code.notes: Generated phase-parent scope text contradicts private-vocabulary cleanup requirement.

checklist_evidence.notes: No applied/T-*.md files were present; sampled spec and implementation-summary claims.

resource_map_coverage.notes: Manifest/resolver/renderer/scaffolder/invariance surfaces sampled; legacy template dirs absent.

overlay.notes: Workflow-invariance test does not exercise generated phase-parent body text.

findingDetails.title: Phase-parent scaffolding still emits private manifest vocabulary into generated public spec text
findingDetails.evidence: create.sh renders the phase-parent template and substitutes scope rows containing child phase manifest, while the spec requires private vocabulary cleanup and generated public surfaces to remain workflow-invariant.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace the generated scope text with taxonomy-neutral wording such as child phase list or phase map, and extend workflow-invariance coverage to rendered phase-parent output.
findingDetails.scopeProof: rg over create.sh, phase-parent template, workflow-invariance test, agents, and command surfaces found this generated-output substitution site.

findingsNew.claim: workflow-invariance.vitest.ts scans public AI-facing surfaces but path-allowlists whole default public roots before asserting no banned vocabulary remains.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether the allowance was scoped to maintainer-only files or fixtures; separate narrow allowances exist, but isLegacyPhaseCleanupDebt broadly covers .opencode/agents/, .opencode/commands/, root policy docs, and SKILL.md. Sampled current surfaces with rg; current hits were not required to prove the false-negative path.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The allowance may be temporary cleanup debt, but the final implementation summary and resource map mark these surfaces as cleaned and directly scanned by the invariance test.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if these paths are intentionally reclassified as out-of-scope or another required gate scans them without the broad allowlist.

spec_code.notes: REQ-002/SC-003 require public-surface workflow invariance; the test suppresses whole default public roots.

checklist_evidence.notes: No applied/T-*.md directory exists; used spec/resource-map/implementation-summary/test evidence.

resource_map_coverage.notes: Sampled resolver, renderer, scaffolder, validator, and workflow-invariance surfaces; new gap is workflow-invariance gate correctness.

overlay.notes: Agent/command public surfaces are collected but path-allowlisted before assertion.

findingDetails.title: Workflow-invariance test path-allowlists the public surfaces it claims to gate
findingDetails.evidence: collectDefaultSurfaces includes .opencode/command, .opencode/agent, and SKILL.md, but isLegacyPhaseCleanupDebt marks those same public roots allowed and isAllowedHit drops every non-extra hit before the final empty-hit assertion.
findingDetails.recommendation: Remove the broad public-root allowlist or replace it with narrow documented exceptions, then add a default-public-surface sentinel proving banned vocabulary fails inside .opencode/agent or .opencode/commands/spec_kit.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The allowlist is centralized and applies to all file hits under the listed public roots; extra-path sentinel coverage does not exercise default public paths suppressed by isLegacyPhaseCleanupDebt.

findingsNew.claim: Template-rendering golden coverage can pass while phase-parent scaffold output still contains the create-time private-vocabulary leak it is supposed to guard.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked the phase-parent manifest template, create.sh create-time substitutions, scaffold golden test, and workflow-invariance scan roots. The template source now uses the neutral placeholder, but create.sh inserts child phase manifest after rendering and the golden test only snapshots renderInlineGates output from the raw template.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The snapshot normalization may have been intended as a migration aid, but after the manifest migration it hides regressions in the exact T-122/T-123 cleanup class and does not exercise generated phase-parent scaffold output.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if another mandatory generated-scaffold snapshot runs create.sh for phase parents and fails on the create-time private-vocabulary leak before release.

template_rendering.notes: Raw template rendering is covered, but create-time phase-parent substitutions are not covered by golden snapshots.

spec_code.notes: T-122/T-123 require new manifest outputs to remove legacy private vocabulary, but create.sh can reintroduce it after template rendering.

checklist_evidence.notes: No applied/T-*.md files were present; sampled tasks/spec test surfaces and generated scaffold path instead.

resource_map_coverage.notes: Reviewed manifest phase-parent template, resolver-consumed template path, create.sh generated scaffold path, and stale rendered-output coverage.

findingDetails.title: Golden scaffold snapshots miss create-time phase-parent rendering regressions
findingDetails.evidence: The snapshot test renders phase-parent.spec.md.tmpl directly and normalizes legacy private-vocabulary strings before comparison, while create.sh renders that template and then injects _scope_rows containing child phase manifest into the public scaffold body. The test therefore passes the template snapshot even when generated phase-parent output violates T-123.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a golden/sentinel test that executes create.sh phase-parent scaffolding (or the same post-render substitution function) and asserts the rendered spec.md contains no private vocabulary; remove the migration normalization for T-122/T-123 leak strings from the golden path once manifest templates are the source of truth.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared the raw template test path, create.sh post-render substitution path, and workflow-invariance default scan path; only create.sh generated output contains the missed substitution surface.

findingsNew.claim: Shell validator rules skip manifest-declared lazy docs, creating validator coverage and MCP/shell parity gaps for generated command/agent/workflow-owned docs.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether another shell rule validates lazy docs for source/structure. check-spec-doc-integrity scans only root markdown links/metadata/handover targets and does not perform template-source/header/section checks for lazy docs; a temporary strict-validation probe showed TEMPLATE_SOURCE passing while an existing handover.md lacked a SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE marker.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: Lazy docs may be optional for presence, but once generated they are manifest-defined template outputs with owners and versions. Optional presence does not justify skipping structure/source validation when the file exists.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if maintainers intentionally declare lazy docs out of scope for shell validator source/structure checks and add equivalent MCP-only release criteria.

spec_code.notes: REQ-004/SC-005 require validator reliability, but shell validators omit manifest-declared lazy docs that MCP validation includes.

checklist_evidence.notes: No applied/T-*.md files were present; sampled spec, tasks, resource map, manifest, shell validators, and MCP validator.

resource_map_coverage.notes: Validator/public-surface entries sampled; no applied target-file list existed to perform touched-vs-map diff.

overlay.notes: Command/agent/workflow-owned lazy docs are generated cross-runtime surfaces but not covered by shell source/header/section validators.

findingDetails.title: Shell validator rules skip manifest-declared lazy docs
findingDetails.evidence: The manifest declares lazyAddonDocs for handover.md, debug-delegation.md, and research/research.md, but the shell helper docs mode only returns requiredCoreDocs plus requiredAddonDocs. check-template-source.sh, check-template-headers.sh, and check-sections.sh all iterate that reduced list, while MCP collectDocuments includes lazyAddonDocs plus resource-map.md/context-index.md.
findingDetails.recommendation: Split required-presence validation from present-file structure validation: keep check-files on required docs, but let template-source/header/section rules inspect existing manifest-declared lazy/optional docs as well, matching the MCP validator's document set.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The reduced helper list feeds three shell rules; MCP validation uses a broader resolver-derived document set, so the issue affects shell strict validation and runtime parity rather than one isolated rule.

findingsNew.claim: The create agent remains on the old CAPABILITY SCAN heading while the rest of the exposed agent mirror set has moved to ROUTING SCAN, leaving an AI-facing runtime surface inconsistent with the ADR-005 cleanup.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Searched the scoped agent directory for CAPABILITY SCAN and ROUTING SCAN; only create.md retained the old heading among scanned routing sections. Checked root runtime mirrors to confirm @create is an exposed AI-facing agent, not a private orphan.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The create agent may have been added after the original audit list, but .opencode/agents/ is in the review scope and root mirrors advertise @create, so it inherits the same public-surface vocabulary requirement.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if maintainers explicitly classify .opencode/agents/create.md as outside AI-facing public-surface cleanup despite the root mirrors exposing @create, or if capability vocabulary is intentionally allowed for agent routing headings.

agent_cross_runtime.notes: Root runtime mirrors expose @create, but the .opencode/agents/create.md prompt was not synchronized with the agent heading vocabulary used by the rest of the prompt set.

spec_code.notes: ADR-005/workflow-invariance cleanup requires public AI-facing surfaces to avoid private taxonomy vocabulary, and this AI-facing prompt still uses the old capability heading.

resource_map_coverage.notes: No applied/T-*.md files were present to cross-check; direct scope audit shows the resource-map agent table omits an exposed agent file under the scoped .opencode/agents/ directory.

overlay.notes: Agent mirrors are mostly synchronized, but the create-agent mirror remains stale.

findingDetails.title: Create agent prompt was missed by the cross-runtime agent vocabulary cleanup
findingDetails.evidence: The resource map says all AI-facing agent definitions under .opencode/agents/ are in scope for the heading rename, but omits create.md from the enumerated agent cleanup list. AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md expose @create as a first-class agent, and create.md still contains ## 2. CAPABILITY SCAN while sibling agent prompts use ROUTING SCAN.
findingDetails.recommendation: Rename the create.md section heading to ROUTING SCAN and add .opencode/agents/create.md to the agent cleanup/resource-map coverage so future mirror scans include it.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Directory-wide heading scan found the old heading in create.md and routing headings in the other sampled agent prompts; root instruction mirrors confirm the missed file is reachable through the same agent surface.
